get.summary: `GET /agents` — list the agent registry keys a routine may target.

post.summary: `POST /echo` — parse a JSON body and return the message with a server timestamp.

get.description:
Returns the name this daemon uses to match `machines[]` targeting lists on routines. Useful for
clients (e.g. the UI) that want to default their views to local entries only.

get.summary: `GET /machines` — distinct machine names this daemon knows about.
get.description:
There is no central machine registry, so the "known" set is the union of every `machines`
targeting list declared by a routine, plus this machine's own resolved identity
([`crate::machine::current_machine`]) so the local machine is always pickable even before
anything targets it. Sorted and de-duplicated. Backs the UI machine picker; mirrors the
`moadim machine list` CLI but reads the live in-memory store instead of disk.

post.summary: `POST /restart` — stop this server and start a fresh instance.
post.description:
The running server cannot rebind its own port, so it spawns a detached `moadim restart` helper
that stops it and starts a new process, mirroring the `moadim restart` CLI command and the
`restart` MCP tool. Responds with the helper's PID before the restart completes.

500.description: could not spawn the restart helper

get.summary: `GET /routines` — list routines, optionally filtered and sorted by repository.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description:
Keep only routines with at least one repository whose URL contains this
substring (case-insensitive). Empty or absent keeps every routine.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Field to sort by (default: creation time).

parameters.name: local_only
parameters.description:
When `true`, only return routines whose `machines` list includes the current machine.
Defaults to `false` (return all routines, preserving backwards compatibility).

parameters.name: include_prompts
parameters.description:
When `true`, include each routine's `prompt` in the response. Defaults to `false`:
the prompt (often the largest field) is omitted so listings stay compact. Fetch a
single routine with `svc_get` / `GET /routines/{id}` to always see its prompt.

get.summary: `GET /routines.ics` — iCalendar feed of every enabled routine's upcoming fire times.
get.description:
Returns a `text/calendar` body suitable for subscribing to in an external calendar
(Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, …) so upcoming runs show up alongside other events.
With `?routine=<id>` the feed is scoped to a single routine (named after it); without
it every enabled routine is rendered (issue #263).

parameters.name: routine
parameters.description:
Render only the fire times of the routine with this UUID. Absent (the default)
renders every enabled routine. An unknown or disabled id yields a well-formed
empty calendar.

200.description: iCalendar (text/calendar) feed of upcoming routine fire times

post.summary: `POST /routines/cleanup` — reap finished, expired run workbenches on demand.

get.summary: `GET /routines/lock` — return the current global lock status.

post.summary: `POST /routines/lock` — create a lock sentinel, halting all routine scheduling and triggers.

delete.summary: `DELETE /routines/lock` — remove lock sentinel(s), restoring routine scheduling.

get.summary: `GET /routines/{id}` — retrieve a single routine by UUID.

put.summary: `PUT /routines/{id}` — fully replace a routine (behaves identically to PATCH).

get.summary: `GET /routines/{id}/flags` — list open flags raised against a routine.

post.summary: `POST /routines/{id}/flags` — raise a new flag against a routine.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Flag filename, as returned by create/list

get.summary: `GET /routines/{id}/logs` — return the newest workbench `agent.log` as plain text.

200.description: Log file contents as plain text

post.summary: `POST /routines/{id}/scheduled-trigger` — run a routine on its schedule.
post.description:
The daemon-side endpoint the generated crontab line invokes (`moadim schedule trigger <id>`).
Unlike [`trigger`] it does not record a manual trigger; the spawned command records the scheduled
timestamp itself. See [`svc_trigger_scheduled`].

post.summary: `POST /routines/{id}/trigger` — manually run a routine outside its schedule.
`POST /shutdown` — ask the server to stop gracefully.
post.description:
Used by the UI "STOP" button (and the `moadim stop` command) to kill a backgrounded server that
has no controlling terminal. The response is sent before the graceful shutdown completes.

CleanupResponse.description: Result of an on-demand workbench cleanup sweep.

removed.description: Number of finished, expired run workbenches removed by this sweep.

type.description:
Free-text flag category. Common examples: `"bug"`, `"gap"`, `"edge_case"`, `"question"`,
`"blocker"` — any string is accepted.

CreateRoutineRequest.description: Request body for creating a new routine.

enabled.description: Whether to create the routine enabled (defaults to `true`).

machines.description: Machines to run this routine on (defaults to empty = runs nowhere until assigned).

max_runtime_secs.description:
Max wall-clock seconds a run may execute before the watchdog kills its hung
session. `None` uses the default cap (`MAX_RUNTIME_SECS`).

model.description:
Model ID to run the agent with, or `None` to use the agent's own default. A
blank/whitespace-only value is treated the same as `None`.

repositories.description: Repositories to list as context (defaults to empty).

schedule.description:
Cron expression for the new routine. Evaluated in the host's local system
timezone (the OS crontab timezone), not UTC.

tags.description:
Free-form labels for the routine (defaults to empty). Each entry is trimmed
and must be non-blank.

ttl_secs.description:
Workbench retention in seconds for finished runs; caps the cron-derived
retention lower. `None` uses `min(MAX_TTL_SECS, cron interval)`.

DependencyHealth.description:
External-binary dependencies the daemon relies on at runtime, and whether each is resolvable on
the daemon's `PATH`. Surfaced in [`HealthResponse`] so the UI/CLI can flag a missing dependency
instead of having routine runs silently no-op.

tmux.description: Whether `tmux` (used to launch every routine agent) resolves on the daemon's `PATH`.

timestamp.description: Server timestamp (Unix seconds) when the echo was produced.

Flag.description: A single flag raised against a routine.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) the flag was created.

filename.description: Filename on disk under the routine's `flags/` dir; the handle used to resolve it.

scope.description: Whether this flag is committed (general) or machine-local.

FlagScope.description: Whether a flag file is committed to version control or kept machine-local.

build_date.description: Committer date (`YYYY-MM-DD`) of the build commit, or `"unknown"` outside a git checkout.

dependencies.description: Presence of required external binaries on the daemon's `PATH`.

git_sha.description: Short git commit SHA the daemon was built from, or `"unknown"` outside a git checkout.

machine.description: Resolved name of this machine (from `MOADIM_MACHINE`, `~/.config/moadim/machine.local.toml`, or hostname).

uptime_secs.description: Seconds elapsed since the server started.

LockStatus.description: Current state of both lock sentinels.

local.description: Whether the gitignored `.local.lock` file is present.

locked.description: `true` if either sentinel is present (all routine scheduling and triggers halted).

shared.description: Whether the committed `.lock` file is present.

name.description: Resolved name of this machine (from `MOADIM_MACHINE`, `~/.config/moadim/machine.local.toml`, or hostname).

Repository.description: A git repository made available to a routine's agent as prompt context (not cloned by moadim).

branch.description: Branch to use, or `None` for the remote default branch.

helper_pid.description: PID of the detached helper process performing the stop-old-then-start-new restart.

Routine.description: A persisted routine: a scheduled AI-agent task.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the routine was created.

last_manual_trigger_at.description:
Unix timestamp (seconds) when the routine was last manually triggered, if ever.

Only manual triggers (`trigger_routine`) update this; scheduled cron firings run the built
command directly and do not. Accepts the legacy `last_triggered_at` key on deserialize.

last_scheduled_trigger_at.description:
Unix timestamp (seconds) when the routine was last fired by its cron schedule, if ever.

The mirror of [`Routine::last_manual_trigger_at`] for scheduled runs: a manual trigger
updates only the manual field, a scheduled firing updates only this one. The host OS crontab
line runs `moadim schedule trigger <id>`, and the launch command the daemon spawns stamps this
timestamp into the gitignored `scheduled.local.toml` sidecar at fire time (via its `printf`
step); the daemon reads it back on load. The daemon never writes this field directly (it is
absent from `routine.toml` and the daemon-owned `state.local.toml`), so re-persisting a
routine can't clobber it.

machines.description:
Machines this routine runs on. Each daemon schedules a routine only when this list names its
own machine identity ([`crate::machine::current_machine`]); an **empty list runs nowhere**, so
a routine is dormant until explicitly assigned. Lets one shared config repo drive different
routines on different machines.

max_runtime_secs.description:
Maximum wall-clock seconds a single run may execute before the cleanup watchdog force-kills
its (hung) tmux session, after which the workbench is reaped under the normal TTL rules.
`None` uses `min(MAX_RUNTIME_SECS, cron interval)`; an explicit value can only lower that. A
session still within this bound is never touched. The cap and
[`Routine::effective_max_runtime_secs`] live in the cleanup module.

model.description:
Model ID to run the agent with (e.g. `"claude-sonnet-4-6"`), passed as `--model` on the
agent invocation. `None` uses the agent's own default.

prompt.description:
The task prompt handed to the agent.

Omitted from serialized output when empty. A persisted routine always has a
non-blank prompt (enforced by `validate_prompt`), so this never affects
`routine.toml` persistence; it lets list responses drop the prompt by blanking
it in-memory (see [`RoutineListQuery::include_prompts`] / `svc_list`).

repositories.description: Repositories listed in the prompt as context.

schedule.description:
Cron expression defining when the routine runs, evaluated in the host's local
system timezone (the OS crontab timezone), not UTC.

source.description: `"managed"` for routines owned by this server.

tags.description:
Free-form labels for grouping and filtering routines (e.g. `"triage"`, `"nightly"`).
Defaults to empty; each entry is trimmed and must be non-blank.

title.description: Human name; slugified to name the workbench and tmux session.

ttl_secs.description:
How long (seconds) a finished run's workbench is retained before auto-cleanup removes it.
Caps the cron-derived retention (`min(MAX_TTL_SECS, cron interval)`) lower; it can only
shorten, never extend it. `None` uses the cron-derived value. Sessions still running are
never reaped. The cap and [`Routine::effective_ttl_secs`] live in the cleanup module.

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the routine was last updated.

agent_command_available.description:
`true` if the agent config's `command` (e.g. `claude`, `codex`) resolves to an executable
on the daemon's `PATH`. Distinct from [`Self::agent_registered`]: a routine can have a
present, well-formed agent config yet reference a binary that isn't installed, in which
case the cron firing launches a tmux session that dies immediately with "command not
found" — a silent no-op indistinguishable from a healthy routine by `agent_registered`
alone. `false` whenever the agent config is missing, unreadable, or malformed, since no
`command` can be resolved in that case either.

agent_registered.description: `true` if an agent config exists at `~/.config/moadim/agents/<agent>.toml`.

file_path.description: Absolute path to the routine's `routine.toml` file on disk.

flag_count.description:
Number of open flags raised against this routine (see [`super::flags`]). Surfaced here so
listings can badge it without a separate `list_flags` round-trip per routine.

schedule_description.description:
Human-readable description of the schedule, including the timezone the
cron expression is interpreted in, or `null` if it cannot be parsed.

timezone.description:
IANA name of the local timezone the schedule is interpreted in (e.g.
`"Asia/Jerusalem"`), or `null` if it cannot be determined. Cron
expressions are evaluated in this timezone, **not** UTC.

RoutineResponse.description: A [`Routine`] enriched with derived, non-persisted fields for API responses.

RoutineSort.description: Field to sort a routine listing by.

name.description: New machine name. Trimmed; must be non-empty.

SortOrder.description: Sort direction for a routine listing.

UpdateRoutineRequest.description: Request body for partially updating an existing routine.

agent.description: New agent key, or `None` to keep the existing value.

enabled.description: New enabled state, or `None` to keep the existing value.

machines.description: New machines targeting list, or `None` to keep the existing value.

max_runtime_secs.description: New max runtime (seconds) for a single run, or `None` to keep the existing value.

model.description:
New model ID, or `None` to keep the existing value. A blank/whitespace-only value clears
the model back to the agent's own default.

prompt.description: New prompt, or `None` to keep the existing value.

repositories.description: New repositories list, or `None` to keep the existing value.

schedule.description:
New cron expression, or `None` to keep the existing value. Evaluated in the
host's local system timezone (the OS crontab timezone), not UTC.

tags.description: New tags list, or `None` to keep the existing value.

title.description: New title, or `None` to keep the existing value.

ttl_secs.description: New workbench TTL (seconds), or `None` to keep the existing value.
